arts.ttl: Report: Arsenal targeting £42m player who is having a 'disaster' season if they sell Jorginho this month
arts.sum: As we edge closer to the end of the January transfer window, Arsenal are seemingly upping their efforts to improve the options available to Mikel...

arts.ttl: 'This is the expectation': Fabrizio Romano shares what Arsenal are 'highly likely' to do in the window now
'It has been in my head': Mikel Arteta says he's actually thinking of changing one Arsenal player's position
How Arsenal would line up with Matheus Cunha
arts.sum: Ben White is the club’s preferred right-back when fully fit, with William Saliba, Gabriel Magalhaes and Jurrien Timber also favoured by Mikel Arteta. Arsenal’s lack of a No.10 means the midfield trio of Martin Odegaard, Declan Rice and Thomas Partey will remain untouched, while Cunha will be deployed in an attacking role.

arts.ttl: Chelsea set sights on £66m Arsenal target as ambitious swap deal takes shape
arts.sum: Dusan Vlahovic could leave Juventus if the price is right and Chelsea might be keen should the Serie A giants meet their asking price for defender Renato Veiga

arts.ttl: Matheus Cunha to Arsenal transfer takes twist as Premier League rivals plot audacious bid
arts.sum: Wolves forward Matheus Cunha is attracting interest from a number of Premier League clubs and Nottingham Forest have now emerged as a contender as they look to boost their top four bid

arts.ttl: Arsenal receive Mbeumo transfer green light after Forest make Cunha decision
arts.sum: football.london says: While Arteta will certainly want an attacking addition imminently, this set of transfer dominos could set Arsenal up well to sign Mbeumo in the summer. With that being said, football.london has rounded up the latest Arsenal transfer news and rumours during the January window.

arts.ttl: Arsenal transfer news: Gunners suffer Dusan Vlahovic blow amid £67m Benjamin Sesko dilemma
arts.sum: Arsenal have less than two weeks to bolster Mikel Arteta's squad before the January transfer window closes, with the Gunners being urged to sign a new striker amid their fading Premier League title hopes

arts.ttl: Report: Nottingham Forest make Arsenal target their top January priority
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest have reportedly made Wolves forward Matheus Cunha their top priority for the January transfer window as they aim to strengthen their squad and push for a top-four finish. The City Ground club are eager to sign a new attacker before the transfer deadline, with the Daily Mail revealing that Cunha is identified as […] The post Report: Nottingham Forest make Arsenal target their top January priority appeared first on CaughtOffside.
